Title: ANAC005 is a membrane-associated transcription factor and regulates vascular development in Arabidopsis

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Transcription factor; membrane-associated NAC family transcription factor
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: xylem / vascular tissue of inflorescence stem
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mechanical strength and vascular transport support
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased ANAC005 expression reduces xylem differentiation and decreases lignin content; dominant repressor ANAC005-SRDX increases xylem development and lignin content
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative regulator of lignin accumulation associated with xylem differentiation in vascular development
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CAD4; CCOAOMT1; COMT1; FAH1; EXPA9; ANT; CESA1; CESA3; CESA6; CESA4; CESA8
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ANAC005 is expressed mainly in vascular tissues, especially developing xylem/tracheary element-associated cells. 2. Overexpression of ANAC005 causes dwarfism, reduced xylem differentiation, and reduced lignin content. 3. ANAC005 represses lignin biosynthetic gene CAD4 and affects additional vascular-development genes. 4. ANAC005-SRDX produces opposite effects, increasing xylem development and lignin accumulation. 5. ANAC005 protein is plasma membrane-associated, and deletion of the C-terminal 20 aa causes nuclear localization.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter-GUS reporter assay; qRT-PCR; transgenic overexpression; dominant repressor fusion (SRDX); stem section microscopy; lignin quantification; GFP subcellular localization in Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially expressed in vascular tissues, especially xylem and developing tracheary element-adjacent cells; weak expression in phloem
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mainly in vascular tissues of cotyledons, rosette leaves, petioles, inflorescence stem, cauline leaves, petals, and stigma; high in xylem/developing tracheary element region
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation; acts as a transcription activator in vascular development context but overall negatively affects lignin accumulation, likely via activating a repressor of lignin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses CAD4 expression in ANAC005 overexpression lines; dominant repressor increases CAD4, CCOAOMT1, COMT1, and FAH1; overexpression downregulates cambium/primary wall genes and upregulates secondary wall genes CESA4 and CESA8
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Lignin biosynthesis associated with xylem differentiation and secondary wall development
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: KNAT7 (proposed in discussion, not experimentally validated)
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAC family; NAM domain with A-E motifs; C-terminal transcription activation region; membrane-association determinant in C-terminal 20 aa
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane; truncated forms lacking C-terminal 20 aa localize to nucleus
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native ANAC005 promoter; CaMV 35S constitutive promoter
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard growth at 22°C under long-day white light; salt treatment reported to have no effect on localization
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dwarf phenotype, reduced cell elongation, altered leaf morphology, altered inflorescence/pedicel angle
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering vascular development and lignin deposition, but reduced growth and dwarfism are major cautions
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Tissue-specific microarray analysis referenced from previous study; no accession reported in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Primary study focus is vascular development rather than nutrient quality. Lignin is the only metabolism-related terminal product directly measured. Membrane association is unusual for NAC proteins and mediated by a noncanonical basic C-terminal motif rather than a typical hydrophobic TM domain.
